title: Taloron
content:

{{Quote|Crutag's a Taloron Hunter. He ain't gonna give up on us.|A Rebel Alliance operative|<ref name="CAV2" />}}
The '''Talorons''' were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]], [[Color/Legends|blue]]-skinned [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]] native to the [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]] [[Taloron system/Legends|Taloron system]]. For much of their existence, the Talorons were peaceful [[Farm/Legends|farmers]] and [[Merchant/Legends|merchants]]. However, when [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] groups set up operations in the Taloron [[Star system/Legends|system]] and began preying upon its peaceful denizens, the Talorons were forced to regroup and militarize, lest they be wiped out completely from the predations. The resulting war, the [[Taloron Pirate Conflict]], completely reshaped Taloron society and birthed the infamous [[Taloron Hunters]], distinguished by their trademark [[Taloron Hunter armor|armor]].

Once the Talorons had driven the pirates from their system, many members of the species turned their strong reverence for law and order toward careers as [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] and [[Assassin/Legends|assassins]]. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], members of the species split their loyalties: the Taloron Hunter known as [[Crutag]], for example, supported the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and pursued [[Bounty/Legends|bounties]] on members of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], while the Hunter known as [[Korgath]] supported the Rebels as a member of the [[Alliance Special Operations/Legends|Special Operations]] [[Scandium Team]]. Another Taloron, known by the alias [["Blue" Imcrix]], [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggled]] [[Spice/Legends|spice]] after the rise of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. The Talorons had access to [[Starship/Legends|starship]] technology and manufactured the craft known as the [[Taloron Prey Chaser]].

==Biology and appearance==
Talorons were [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]], [[Color/Legends|blue]]-skinned [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]] with a physiology similar to that of [[Human/Legends|Humans]]: a head, four [[Limb/Legends|limbs]], and five-fingered hands. Members of the [[Species/Legends|species]] had sunken features and small [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. While some Talorons were tall and thin with broad shoulders,<ref name="CAV2" /> others were short. The species had both [[Gender/Legends|male and female]] members. A sixty-seven-[[Standard year/Legends|year]]-old Taloron was considered middle-aged.<ref name="CRO">{{JournalCite|11|Cracken's Rebel Operatives (article)}}</ref>

==Society and culture==
{{Quote|If they are law abiding citizens, then I wouldn't need to discipline them, would I?|Crutag|<ref name="CAV2" />}}
[[File:Taloron_Hunter_armor.png|thumb|left|Taloron Hunter armor featured a wide helmet and plates.]]
Talorons valued order, a penchant that reached the point of zealous obsession. For much of their history, they were a peaceful people, with most Talorons earning their living as [[Farm/Legends|farmers]] or [[Merchant/Legends|merchants]]. However, the hardships of the [[Taloron Pirate Conflict]] reshaped them into a warrior culture.<ref name="CAV2" /> The species' native [[Language/Legends|language]] was known as [[Taloron language|Taloron]];<ref name="AIR" /> Talorons could learn to speak and understand [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]] as well.<ref name="CAV2" />

After their society had been reforged through war, the organization known as the [[Taloron Hunters]] came into being. Members of this group were known as callous and single-minded [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] and [[Assassin/Legends|assassins]],<ref name="CAV2" /> well-known in less reputable corners of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] as aggressive, testy predators.<ref name="CRO" /> Their reputation was such that the [[Ocsin]]in [[Unarmed combat/Legends|martial artist]] [[Cene Gilvent]], a member of the [[Zulirian Swordmasters]], wielded a three-bladed [[Sword/Legends|sword]] named [[Soknar]], the Taloron word for ''several''.<ref name="AIR" /> Taloron Hunters were noted for [[Taloron Hunter armor|their distinctive armor]], which included a padded undersuit, [[Armor/Legends|armor]] plating, a wide helmet with two thin projections to the sides, a hip pouch, and a cylindrical [[Backpack/Legends|backpack]]. Typical armaments for the class included a [[Blaster carbine/Legends|blaster carbine]] with a [[Vibrobayonet/Legends|vibrobayonet]] and a standard sword.<ref name="SWSBb 97">[[The Star Wars Sourcebook (Second Edition)|''The Star Wars Sourcebook'', Second Edition]]</ref> Other galactic toughs adopted this style, such as a Human crony of the [[Aleena/Legends|Aleena]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Sollima]].<ref name="Hollow moon">''[[Star Wars Adventures: Han Solo and the Hollow Moon of Khorya]]''</ref>

The Talorons had access to technology on par with much of the rest of the galaxy. They manufactured their own [[Starship/Legends|starships]], including the [[Taloron Prey Chaser]]. This vessel was forty [[Meter/Legends|meters]] long and, although it required only a single [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]], could hold up to twenty-five passengers.<ref name="CAV2" />

==History==
The Talorons hailed from the [[Taloron system/Legends|Taloron system]] in [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]].<ref name="EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> They developed a peaceful society made up largely of traders and farmers.<ref name="CAV2" />

Over [[Time/Legends|time]], bands of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] began moving into the Taloron [[Star system/Legends|system]] under the leadership of a ruthless pirate [[Captain/Legends|chief]] named [[Halgir]]. The brigands primarily preyed upon the Talorons, whom Halgir cowed into acceding to his demands. By several years before the outbreak of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the system had been overrun by bandits, and the Talorons realized that they could no longer tolerate Halgir's oppression. In a conflict dubbed the Taloron Pirate Conflict, the system's native denizens turned on their pirate overlords, using farming tools as weapons or fighting with their bare hands. Halgir and his forces responded by razing [[City/Legends|villages]] and [[Death/Legends|killing]] anyone who opposed them. At the peak of the hostilities, the Talorons fell to the brink of extinction.<ref name="CAV2" />

Nevertheless, the surviving Talorons regrouped, their determination only hardened by Halgir's pillaging. They retooled their [[Army/Legends|army]] into a guerrilla force that resisted the pirates with tactics that emphasized ambushes and hit-and-run assaults. The elite warriors known as the Taloron Hunters also formed at this time. The Taloron Pirate Conflict continued through the fall of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the rise of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], before the Talorons finally defeated Halgir. They captured him alive and presented him to the Imperial authorities in their region.<ref name="CAV2" />

Despite the end of the war, Taloron society did not return to its previous state of peaceful trade and agriculture. The conflict had remolded their culture into one based on warfare and violence. As a result, several Talorons left their home system to find work as [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] hunters, channeling their intolerance toward pirates into campaigns against pirates and criminals throughout the galaxy.<ref name="CAV2" /> Decades later, by [[137 ABY]], the Taloron system had fallen into a region of space influenced by the [[Chiss Ascendancy/Legends|Chiss Ascendancy]], a society of blue-skinned humanoids known as the [[Chiss/Legends|Chiss]].<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref>

==Talorons in the galaxy==
[[File:Korgath_SWJ11.jpg|thumb|right|Korgath was a Taloron Rebel Alliance infiltrator who traded on the Taloron Hunters' fierce reputation.]]
After the resolution of the Taloron Pirate Conflict, many Talorons coupled their newfound martial prowess with their species' natural desire for law and order to find work as bounty hunters. These Talorons plied the [[Hyperspace route/Legends|spacelanes]] apprehending pirates and other criminals. One such Taloron was [[Crutag]], a Taloron Hunter and a veteran of the Pirate Conflict who left soon after its conclusion. After a stint with the [[Bounty Hunters' Guild/Legends|Bounty Hunters' Guild]] house known as the the [[Ragnar Syndicate]],<ref name="CAV2" /><ref name="GG10">''[[Galaxy Guide 10: Bounty Hunters]]''</ref> Crutag left the organization with his Taloron Prey Chaser, the ''[[Scent of Fear]]''. He gathered a diverse crew, which at one point included a [[Unidentified Taloron lieutenant|Taloron lieutenant]], who eventually died on a hunt. Although Crutag boasted that he had never failed to apprehend a target, he was in reality only a moderately successful bounty hunter. Nevertheless, his reputation was one of single-minded determination and violent cruelty to his targets. Although he publicly professed loyalty to only himself, Crutag believed that the Galactic Empire was the rightful government of the galaxy.<ref name="CAV2" />

Between [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]] and [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]],<ref name="date">Based on the appearance of Rebel Alliance agents in ''Domain of Evil'', the adventure must occur after the formation of the Alliance, which happened in 2 BBY, as established in ''[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed]]''. In ''The Dark Forces Saga, Part 2'', it is mentioned that Qu Rahn heard a report of the death of Halagad Ventor—who dies during the events of ''Domain of Evil''—before he contacted [[Morgan Katarn]]. This implies that Ventor died before Morgan Katarn, whose own death occurred in 1 BBY, per ''[[Dark Forces: Soldier for the Empire]]''.</ref> Crutag learned of a bounty on a group of [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] operatives. The Taloron chased the Rebels from [[Kwenn Space Station/Legends|Kwenn Space Station]] to the nearby [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Trinta]]. When the Rebels crash-landed, Crutag and his minions gave pursuit. However, they encountered strange apparitions that cost the lives of several hunters. Unbeknownst to them, these were [[Mind trick/Legends|Force illusions]] created by the fallen [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Halagad Ventor]]. Crutag did not realize that he had come to the attention of Ventor, who wanted to seduce and corrupt the Taloron to be his [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark-side]] minion. Nevertheless, the Rebels managed to escape the Taloron Hunter.<ref name="CAV2" />

[[File:Crutag.jpg|thumb|left|Crutag was a Taloron Hunter known for his single-minded pursuit of his quarry, if not for his results.]]
Another Taloron Hunter, named [[Korgath]], joined the Rebel Alliance during the Galactic Civil War. The Taloron served as an Alliance [[Infiltrators/Legends|Infiltrator]], eventually joining [[Scandium Team]], one of the Rebellion's earliest [[Alliance Special Operations/Legends|Special Operations]] units, to take his fight to the Empire. Even after Scandium Team disbanded, Korgath officially maintained his position as an Infiltrator, even if his missions tended to be more like those of a Special Operations [[Soldier/Legends|soldier]]. Although friends described him as kind and friendly, Korgath wore Taloron Hunter armor and—though shorter than most Talorons—cultivated a fierce reputation, capitalizing on the widespread perception that Taloron Hunters were callous and violent.<ref name="CRO" />

After the fall of the Empire, the Taloron [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] and [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] known as [["Blue" Imcrix]] ran shipments of [[Rokna Blue|rokna blue]], a kind of [[Spice/Legends|spice]] derived from [[Rokna tree fungus|rokna fungus]] found on the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]].<ref name="EATMS">{{GamerCite|9|Endor and the Moddell Sector}}</ref> Imcrix eventually gave up drug running. Nevertheless, soon after the outbreak of the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]],<ref name="CSWE II 85">{{CSWECite|II|85|Imcrix, "Blue"}}</ref> the Taloron learned that an old spice-processing center named [[Rokna Station]] housed a new strain of rokna blue that had been slow-aged since the drug fell out of popularity years earlier, after the [[Black Fleet Crisis]].<ref name="EATMS" /> Imcrix brought the [[Space station/Legends|station's]] [[Slave circuit|slave-rigged]] security and weapons systems back online in a bid to lure the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] to the site.<ref name="CSWE II 85" /> The plan worked, and a [[New Republic Security Force]] team arrived to disable the ancient station to prevent its weapons systems from harming innocent bystanders.<ref name="WTK">{{GamerCite|9|Wired to Kill}}</ref> However, Imcrix hijacked their starship and took them hostage, intending to use them as bargaining chips when further New Republic forces arrived and attempted to destroy Rokna Station<ref name="CSWE II 85" /> and to help him fight through the reactivated weapons systems to reach the spice harvest.<ref name="WTK" /> In the end, "Blue" Imcrix was captured by his own hostages, who turned him over to the authorities to await trial.<ref name="CSWE II 85" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Pale-skinned_Taloron_Hunter.jpg|thumb|upright|150px|right|A figure in Taloron Hunter armor appears in ''Star Wars Adventures Volume 1''.]]
Authors [[Bill Slavicsek]], [[Bill Smith]], and [[Curtis Smith]] created the Taloron Hunters for ''[[The Star Wars Sourcebook]]'', published by [[West End Games]] in [[1987]] for ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. The authors based the group's appearance and standard equipment on a previously unused production sketch for one of the ''[[Star Wars]]'' feature films.<ref>According to ''The Star Wars Sourcebook'' all artwork in the volume is reused production artwork from [[Lucasfilm]]</ref> The book makes no indication whether ''Taloron'' designates a distinct species.<ref name="SWSBa 98">''[[The Star Wars Sourcebook]]''</ref> The Taloron Hunter named Crutag features prominently in the West End Games adventure ''[[Domain of Evil]]'', written by [[Jim Bambra]] and published in [[1991]]. The background description for Crutag indicates that he has blue skin, the first source to mark the Talorons as [[Alien/Legends|non-Human]].<ref name="CAV2" /> With the republishing of ''Domain of Evil'' as part of ''[[Classic Adventures: Volume Two]]'' in [[1995]], editor [[Eric S. Trautmann]] greatly expanded on Crutag's background, detailing the history of the Talorons in general as well—although his revised description omits any mention of skin color. [[Cracken's Rebel Operatives (article)|''Cracken's Rebel Operatives'']], written by [[Craig Robert Carey|C. Robert Carey]] and [[Trevor J. Wilson]] and published in [[1996]] as part of [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 11|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 11]], includes the first unambiguous indication that the Talorons are a species and not a race of Humans.<ref name="CRO" />

According to the version of ''Domain of Evil'' in ''Classic Adventures: Volume Two'', the Taloron system is located in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="CAV2" /> However, the [[Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion|''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion]] instead places it in Wild Space.

The [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] ''[[Star Wars Adventures: Han Solo and the Hollow Moon of Khorya]]'', published by [[Dark Horse Comics]] in [[2009]], features a character wearing Taloron Hunter armor. The figure, drawn by [[Rick Lacy]] and colored by [[Michael Atiyeh]], has pale skin like a Human rather than blue skin, like Crutag. The character's species is not mentioned, although this article assumes the figure is not a Taloron due to the skin-color discrepancy.{{Fact}}
